codeReview.summary: The fix feature stays within its evidence-only scope and adequately closes the prior scrutiny failure. The new handoff artifact and worker-transcripts entry now exist for worker session c92a43e8-2ded-4e29-b043-67da55b3a218, the handoff explicitly links back to implementation commit ef275044a3136df8b6ba29f28a843b8de2e98257, and the declared hydration regressions were re-run against that landed implementation rather than creating a second implementation claim. I found no remaining code or evidence defects in this revalidation feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill does not explicitly describe an evidence-only revalidation path for already-landed features, so the worker had to mark procedure deviation even though avoiding code changes was the correct in-scope behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-23T20-25-30-486Z__milestone-c-reconnect-public-id-hydration-normalization-evidence-revalidation__c92a43e8-2ded-4e29-b043-67da55b3a218.json records followedProcedure=false only because the task repaired mission evidence for already-landed commit ef275044 without adding tests or implementation changes.

summary: Pass. The fix review confirms the original failure was purely missing scrutiny evidence, and this follow-up supplies that evidence correctly: mission handoff and transcript skeleton now exist, they point to implementation commit ef275044a3136df8b6ba29f28a843b8de2e98257, and the three declared hydration regressions were revalidated without broadening implementation scope.
